Te Pāti Māori — Policy (current, from maoriparty.org.nz/policy)

HOUSING — What we'll do: Build and support more public and affordable homes. Make it easier to build homes on Māori/ancestral land. Stop speculation and land-banking (including action on vacant properties). Why it matters: A warm, safe, secure home is not a luxury. Our people deserve homes, not waiting lists, overcrowding, or speculation.

HEALTH — What we'll do: Re-build and strengthen Māori-led health authority and solutions. Shift the system toward prevention and whānau wellbeing, not just crisis response. Make services accessible, culturally safe, and accountable to Māori. Why it matters: A health system that does not understand Māori will keep failing Māori. Our people deserve care that sees the whole person, strengthens whānau, prevents harm.

EDUCATION & TRAINING — What we'll do: Build a Māori-led education authority and solutions. Embed mātauranga Māori properly across learning. Remove barriers that block whānau from education and training. Support Māori governance and decision-making in education. Why it matters: Education should strengthen identity, grow confidence, open pathways, and prepare our people to lead in the world as Māori.

TAX — What we'll do: Make sure the wealthiest pay their fair share. Use that revenue to fund housing, health, education and whānau support. Why it matters: When the system rewards the top and squeezes everyone else, poverty becomes permanent. Tax is one of the ways we return power, resources, and opportunity back to the people.

ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT — What we'll do: Build Māori-led institutions and funds that grow Māori enterprise. Support trade, innovation, and long-term Māori wealth building. Protect Māori data and knowledge (data sovereignty). Why it matters: Economic power is political power. This is about restoring what was taken and growing Māori wealth.

COST OF LIVING — What we'll do: Lift incomes so people can live with dignity. Strengthen welfare so whānau aren't punished for being poor. Back practical cost-of-living relief (including transport affordability). Why it matters: No whānau should be forced to choose between kai, rent, power, transport, medicine, or school costs.

JUSTICE — What we'll do: Build a Māori-led justice authority and community solutions. Reform laws that criminalise poverty and survival. Move away from mass imprisonment and toward long-term transformation. Why it matters: True justice means ending the machinery that criminalises poverty, trauma, and survival, and building Māori-led solutions that restore mana, safety, and futures.

CLIMATE — What we'll do: Protect whenua and moana from extractive industries (including banning seabed mining). Invest in climate resilience for communities most at risk. Back regenerative and sustainable approaches aligned with Māori values. Why it matters: Climate justice is about protecting our right to live safely, affordably, and sovereign on our own whenua.

FRESHWATER — What we'll do: Assert Māori rights and authority over freshwater. Stop decisions being made without Māori consent. Ensure resources are protected and benefits are shared fairly. Why it matters: Water is life. Decisions about wai must sit with those who have carried responsibility for it.
